question: What is the quarterly copay for a semi-private room under the Community Choice plan before the deductible is applied, given that the quarterly copay for a transplant at a Center Center or Designated Hospital under the same plan is the same amount?
predicted_answer: Community Choice Non-Community Choice At a Quality Center or Designated | $275 quarterly copay, then $275 quarterly copay, then Hospital for transplants deductible, then 100% deductible, then 100% At another hospital $275 quarterly copay, then $750 per-admission copay, then deductible, then 80% deductible, then 80% A case manager is available to support you and your family before the transplant procedure and throughout the recovery period.

Transplants 3: Find out what’s covered Transplants Benefits are payable — subject to any deductible, copays, coinsurance and benefit limits — for necessary medical and surgical expenses incurred for the transplanting of a human organ. To get the highest benefit, see “Quality Centers and Designated Hospitals for transplants” later in this section. Community Choice Non-Community Choice At a Quality Center or Designated | $275 quarterly copay, then $275 quarterly copay, then Hospital for transplants deductible, then 100% deductible, then 100% At another hospital $275 quarterly copay, then $750 per-admission copay, then deductible, then 80% deductible, then 80% A case manager is available to support you and your family before the transplant procedure and throughout the recovery period. If the hospital stay is cancelled or postponed after the tests are performed, the charges will still be covered as long as the physician presents a satisfactory medical explanation. 4. Human organ donor services Benefits are payable — subject to any deductible, copays, coinsurance and benefit limits — for necessary expenses incurred for delivery of a human organ (any part of the human body, excluding blood and blood plasma) and medical expenses incurred by a person in direct connection with the donation of an organ. These are the services and supplies that a facility ordinarily renders for diagnosis or treatment during your hospital stay, including: Q Administration of infusions and transfusions Q Devices that are an integral part of a surgical procedure such as hip joints, skull plates and pacemakers Q Drugs, medications, solutions, biological preparations, and medical and surgical supplies used during your hospital stay Q Tests and exams Q Use of special equipment in the facility Q Use of special rooms, such as operating or treatment rooms If you are admitted to a non-Community Choice hospital from the emergency room, you will be covered at the Community Choice benefit level. 50 Community Choice Plan Handbook (2016-2017)
